title: Liberalism vs conservatism
primary_topics: liberalism vs conservatism in three institutions
sub_topics: Republic v. Ng (February 27, 2024) — foreign divorce recognition
Pulido v. People (July 27, 2021) — bigamy without prior nullity declaration
Article 26(2) of Family Code — purposive interpretation
Second Plenary Council of the Philippines (PCP 2) Acts and Decrees
Pope Francis Singapore September 13, 2024 remarks ('all religions are paths to reach God')
Trump's three SCOTUS appointees (Gorsuch, Kavanaugh, Barrett)
Roe v. Wade (1973) → Dobbs v. Jackson (2022) reversal
2024 US presidential election Harris vs Trump

stances.claim: The Philippine Supreme Court — traditionally a bulwark of conservatism — is on a 'crucial but rocky road to liberalism,' surprising given that 13 of its members are 'insiders' from the judicial ranks expected to be conservative.

Republic v. Ng's liberal interpretation of Article 26(2) is correct — statutes 'cannot always be interpreted literally' and must be read 'according to its spirit or intent'; courts should not 'accept an interpretation that would defeat the intent of the law and its legislators.'

Pope Francis's Singapore remarks ('all religions are paths to reach God… we are all children of God') will 'surely rock the rafters of the Catholic Church' — traditionalists and conservatives who already opposed Vatican II and John Paul II will intensify their opposition.
stances.rhetorical_move: prediction of institutional reaction + ecumenical-framing endorsement

stances.claim: SCOTUS has forked to conservatism — Trump's three appointees flipped the 5-4 balance to 6-3, leading to the Dobbs reversal of Roe; the 2024 US election has become 'an acrimonious combat between liberals and conservatives.'

His fervent hope is that liberalism would not transform into licentiousness (which 'would subvert the rule of law') and conservatism would not convert to authoritarianism like Hitler's Nazism (which 'would violate individual liberty') — Trump's dictator rants are terrifying. 'Heaven forbid!'
stances.rhetorical_move: balanced hedge against both extremes + sharp characterization of Trump

institutions: Supreme Court of the United States
Second Plenary Council of the Philippines
University of the Philippines College of Law

signature_phrases: the bulwarks of conservatism and tradition
a crucial but rocky road to liberalism
rock the rafters of the Catholic Church
an acrimonious combat between liberals and conservatives
liberalism would not transform into licentiousness
conservatism would not convert to authoritarianism
All religions are paths to reach God
register_markers: AN EXAMPLE OF THE COURT'S TREK TO LIBERALISM
THE CATHOLIC CHURCH HAS ALSO BEEN LIBERALIZING
IN CONTRAST, THE LIBERALISM OF THE SCOTUS
decision_framework_signals: applies liberalism-vs-conservatism axis as cross-institutional comparative framework (PH SC / Catholic Church / SCOTUS)
uses purposive (vs literal) interpretation endorsement for Family Code Article 26(2)
uses insider/outsider classification to make structural-judicial predictions
frames healthy middle ground (liberalism without licentiousness, conservatism without authoritarianism) as the rule-of-law-preserving balance
notable_anecdotes: Recalling that 'when I was a student, priests faced the altar during masses, mumbling unintelligible (to the faithful) Latin that frankly turned me off' — pre-Vatican II Catholic formation
Vatican II reforms making the Church more humane and ecumenical, implemented in the Philippines by PCP 2
